html:
Exclusive to:

The Hermit Home is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It can be found on the Hermit Island far out into the Ocean.

The Hermit Home spawns the Crabby Hermit during the day. It can be upgraded three times with material.

  • The first upgrade requires 10 Cookie Cutter Shells, 10 Boards and 1 Fireflies.
  • The second upgrade requires 10 Marble, 5 Cut Stones and 3 Light Bulbs.
  • The third upgrade requires 10 Moon Rocks, 5 Rope and 5 Carpeted Flooring.

A full upgraded Hermit Home is required to obtain the Pearl's Pearl. From level 8 of friendship, the player will address the Hermit Home as Pearl's Place.

Contents

  • 1 Decoration
  • 2 Skins
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Assets
  • 5 Gallery
  • 6 Sounds

Decoration

This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

After relocating Pearl's Place to the mainland with the Hermit Rehomer, it can be upgraded an additional stage with 10 Moon Glass, 10 Driftwood, and 10 Silk. Afterwards, the home has 4 slots for Pearl's Stockings and Bloomers, Wagstaff's Glove, and Wind Charms. Also, player can right click the home to dress Crabby Hermit.

Skins

  • The Event skin does not drop normally. Instead, it is usable by everyone during the official Winter's Feast event.
Event
The Merrymaker Collection
n/a
n/a

Trivia

  • The Hermit Home was introduced in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
  • The music Hermit Home plays before upgrading is actually "Creepy Forest" from the original soundtrack.

"hermithouse"
“It's just an empty shell of a house.”
–Wilson, examining initial Hermit Home.
“What a dump.”
–Willow, examining initial Hermit Home.
“This no place for someone's babushka.”
–Wolfgang, examining initial Hermit Home.
“It's hardly worth saving.”
–Wendy, examining initial Hermit Home.
“HA. IT IS SAD”
–WX-78, examining initial Hermit Home.
“Tsk. Look at the state of it...”
–Wickerbottom, examining initial Hermit Home.
“This place has seen better days.”
–Woodie, examining initial Hermit Home.
“What a sad little hovel.”
–Maxwell, examining initial Hermit Home.
“The öld cröne resides here?”
–Wigfrid, examining initial Hermit Home.
“She must be sad living here all alone...”
–Webber, examining initial Hermit Home.
“That poor old woman... living here all on her own...”
–Warly, examining initial Hermit Home.
“Sad shell...”
–Wormwood, examining initial Hermit Home.
“That can't be up to code.”
–Winona, examining initial Hermit Home.
“What a sad little house for that cranky old louse.”
–Wortox, examining initial Hermit Home.
“Is very nice home.”
–Wurt, examining initial Hermit Home.
“This house could use some fixing up.”
–Walter, examining initial Hermit Home.
“It was a fine home once, but it's fallen into disrepair.”
–Wanda, examining initial Hermit Home.
“It just needed a little love.”
–Wilson, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“I never needed a home, but some people do I guess.”
–Willow, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Make good home for old shell lady.”
–Wolfgang, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Perhaps broken things can be fixed... over time...”
–Wendy, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“IT IS SLIGHTLY LESS MISERABLE LOOKING”
–WX-78, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Improving your living space can do wonders for one's mood.”
–Wickerbottom, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“It looks like a real home now.”
–Woodie, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“It's an improvement.”
–Maxwell, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“The sea witch seems tö be pleased with it.”
–Wigfrid, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“We hope this makes her feel happier!”
–Webber, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“I hope she's a bit more comfortable in there now.”
–Warly, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Happy now”
–Wormwood, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Just needed a bit of elbow grease.”
–Winona, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“A little care can cause remarkable transformations.”
–Wortox, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Crabby lady's house got taller!”
–Wurt, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“A Pinetree Pioneer is always happy to help the elderly!”
–Walter, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
“Time well spent.”
–Wanda, examining upgraded Hermit Home.
The Hermit Home is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It can be found on the Hermit Island far out into the Ocean.
The Hermit Home spawns the Crabby Hermit during the day. It can be upgraded three times with material.
The first upgrade requires 10 Cookie Cutter Shells, 10 Boards and 1 Fireflies.
The second upgrade requires 10 Marble, 5 Cut Stones and 3 Light Bulbs.
The third upgrade requires 10 Moon Rocks, 5 Rope and 5 Carpeted Flooring.
A full upgraded Hermit Home is required to obtain the Pearl's Pearl. From level 8 of friendship, the player will address the Hermit Home as Pearl's Place.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Ngôi Nhà Ẩn Sĩ là một vật thể sinh sản tự nhiên dành riêng cho Don't Starve Together. Nó có thể được tìm thấy trên Đảo Hermit ở xa ngoài Đại dương.
